title: The Weekly Giving Gesture
subtitle: A simple activity for practising generosity without a fixed amount
excerpt: A weekly activity for moving the body through the act of giving before it becomes a calculated decision. Choose one small gesture each week (a coffee paid forward, a book passed on, a meal brought round) and make it without working out the return. The point is the practice of giving freely, not the size of the gesture.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 58 activity in MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use once a week when you want to build the felt experience of giving from a place of ease rather than calculation.

content.text: Once a week, choose one act of giving and do it without calculating what comes back. The giving can be small: a coffee for the person behind you, a book you've finished passed on, a bunch of flowers left on a neighbour's step. The size is not the point. The act is the point — moving through the moment of potential reluctance to arrive at the gesture.
Fear of depletion is most stubborn at the moment of transaction. This activity puts you through that moment repeatedly, at low stakes, until giving from ease becomes familiar.
Pick a day of the week. Monday morning works well as a tone-setter; use whatever fits your routine.
Decide on one gesture before you leave the house. Name it. Write it down if that helps it feel real.
Make the gesture when the moment arrives. Say the affirmation 'giving feels expansive and fearless' before you do it if the reluctance is there.
Don't track the return. Let the giving be complete in itself.
At the end of the week, write one sentence about how it felt to do it. 'Easy', 'harder than I expected', 'nothing' are all valid answers.
After four weeks you'll have a small record of how giving feels in your body. That record is the data — not what came back, but what the giving itself became.
Adapted from Day 58 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Pairs well with the Day 58 tapping practice and affirmation.
